process_name: Invisible rare B -> K*(892) nu nubar decays

evidence: Targeted regex search for B -> K* nu nubar, b -> s nu nubar, Kstar nu, nu_nubar, nunu, and invisible B returned no matches in the required code directories.
quarkConstraints/modern/phenomenology.py:23 lists only epsilon_K, K, B_d, B_s, and D0 as modern phenomenology systems.
quarkConstraints/deltaf2.py:209 defines the legacy Delta F = 2 input set.
quarkConstraints/deltaf2.py:903 and quarkConstraints/deltaf2.py:922 implement B_d and B_s mixing, not Delta B = 1 invisible decays.
flavorConstraints/muToEGamma.py:75 implements check_mu_to_e_gamma; no b -> s nu nubar routine is present.
commands: rg -n "epsilon_K|epsilon_k|Delta m_K|DELTA_M_K|evaluate_delta_mk|B_d|b_d|B_s|b_s|D0|d_mix|mu.?e.?gamma|muToEGamma|BR_LIMIT|MEG" quarkConstraints qcd flavorConstraints neutrinos yukawa warpConfig solvers scanParams tests -g '!*.ipynb'

rg -n "b.?->.?s|X_s|K\\*|Kstar|K\\^\\*|nu.*nubar|nunu|invisible|B_s.*mu|pi.*nu|epsilon.?prime|eps.?prime|t.?->|Z.?->|h.?->|EDM|dipole|conversion|tau.?->|mu.?->.?3e" quarkConstraints qcd flavorConstraints neutrinos yukawa warpConfig solvers scanParams tests -g '!*.ipynb'

rg -n "B\\s*(->|to).*K\\*.*nu|B0.*K\\*|B\\+.*K\\*|Kstar.*nu|K\\*.*nu|K\\^\\*.*nu|b\\s*(->|to)\\s*s\\s*nu|nubar|nu_nubar|nunu|invisible" quarkConstraints qcd flavorConstraints neutrinos yukawa warpConfig solvers scanParams tests -g '!*.ipynb'

theory_context.role: post-2008 SM and EFT reference; not a PDG/equivalent measurement
theory_context.value_summary: SM prediction B(B0 -> K*(892)0 nu nubar) = (9.2 +/- 1.0)e-6

status_history.reason: initial PKA draft deposit for assigned B023 B -> K* nu nubar process
checker batch ca_w5a_beauty found traceability issue
issues: CHK-1: TeX quotes Belle 2017 combined B -> K* nu nubar <2.7e-5 and Buras 2015 SM BR(B0 -> K*0 nu nubar) = (9.2 +/-1.0)e-6, but those values are outside pdg_or_equivalent.

status_history.reason: Cycle-2 writer rework: added the CA-flagged Belle 2017 combined limit and Buras 2015 SM prediction under pdg_or_equivalent.values.
checker batch ca_w5a_beauty_v2 found remaining source-manifest issue
issues: CHK-2: TeX cites BaBar2013:BKstarNuNu, but source_manifest.yaml contains BaBar2013:BKstarNunu; the cited reference key does not resolve.

status_history.reason: Opus arbitration APPROVE-WITH-PATCH per plan v1 :620-622: WA/CA cycle-3 cap reached. CHK-2 is a real but trivial citation-key typo (TeX BaBar2013:BKstarNuNu vs. canonical BaBar2013:BKstarNunu used by B023.yaml experiment_inputs and references/B023/source_manifest.yaml; line 98 of the TeX key-references list already used the canonical lowercase form). Patch applied: B023.tex lines 36 and 55 changed from BaBar2013:BKstarNuNu to BaBar2013:BKstarNunu. All three occurrences now resolve to the manifest key. See flavor_catalog/signoff/by_process/B021_B023.md.

status_history.notes: All measured and quoted prediction value claims verified against fetched source pages or local source snapshots.

sections.pdg_prose:
The canonical current PDG/HFLAV values are upper limits.  HFLAV Dec. 2025,
cross-linked to PDG Live 2025, lists
\[
  \mathcal{B}(B^0\to K^{*0}(892)\nu\bar{\nu}) < 1.8\times 10^{-5},
  \qquad 90\%~{\rm CL},
\]
and
\[
  \mathcal{B}(B^+\to K^{*+}(892)\nu\bar{\nu}) < 4.0\times 10^{-5},
  \qquad 90\%~{\rm CL}.
\]
The neutral limit is from Belle's semileptonic-tag search
(\texttt{Belle2017:BHnunuSemileptonic}); the charged limit is from Belle's
full-sample hadronic-tag search (\texttt{Belle2013:BHnunuHadronic}).  BaBar's
2013 hadronic-tag result is superseded but remains part of the HFLAV input
history (\texttt{BaBar2013:BKstarNunu}).  Local snapshots are under
\texttt{references/B023/}, accessed 2026-05-16.
sections.relevance:
These modes probe the \(\Delta B=1\) FCNC transition \(b\to s\nu\bar{\nu}\).
In RS/anarchic-flavor models they are sensitive to flavor-changing \(Z\),
heavy electroweak-gauge, or other neutral-current couplings generated after
fermion localization and mass-basis rotation.  They are complementary to
\(B_s\) mixing because the final state tests a semileptonic neutral current,
not a \(\Delta F=2\) four-quark amplitude, and the \(K^*\) final state can also
separate left- and right-handed short-distance structures through polarization.
sections.post_2008:
The CFW 2008 baseline predates all current \(B\to K^*\nu\bar{\nu}\) limits.
(\texttt{CsakiFalkowskiWeiler2008:CompositeFlavor}).  Belle's 2013
full-\(\Upsilon(4S)\) hadronic-tag analysis set the charged-mode limit
\(B^+\to K^{*+}\nu\bar{\nu} <4.0\times10^{-5}\)
(\texttt{Belle2013:BHnunuHadronic}).  BaBar's 2013 hadronic-tag result found
no significant excess and remains useful as superseded input history
(\texttt{BaBar2013:BKstarNunu}).  Belle's 2017 semileptonic-tag search improved
the neutral mode to \(<1.8\times10^{-5}\) and quoted a combined vector limit of
\(<2.7\times10^{-5}\) (\texttt{Belle2017:BHnunuSemileptonic}).
Buras--Girrbach-Noe--Niehoff--Straub 2015 updated the SM treatment using form
factors and electroweak corrections, quoting
\(\mathcal{B}(B^0\to K^{*0}\nu\bar{\nu})=(9.2\pm1.0)\times10^{-6}\), and framed
the right-handed-current observables used in later new-physics studies
(\texttt{BurasGirrbachNoeNiehoffStraub2015:BKstarNunu}).
sections.validity:
Experimentally this is a clean missing-energy upper limit, but it is not a
drop-in bound for the present quark scan.  A production constraint needs the
\(b\to s\nu\bar{\nu}\) weak Hamiltonian, \(B\to K^*\) form factors, treatment of
right-handed currents and polarization, and either an experimental likelihood
or a conservative reinterpretation of the published upper limits.
sections.code_coverage_prose:
\textbf{NO.}  Targeted greps over \texttt{quarkConstraints/}, \texttt{qcd/},
\texttt{flavorConstraints/}, \texttt{neutrinos/}, \texttt{yukawa/},
\texttt{warpConfig/}, \texttt{solvers/}, \texttt{scanParams/}, and
\texttt{tests/} found no \(B\to K^*\nu\bar{\nu}\), \(b\to s\nu\bar{\nu}\), or
invisible-\(B\) implementation.  The modern phenomenology surface is limited
to \(\epsilon_K\), \(K\), \(B_d\), \(B_s\), and \(D^0\) at
\texttt{quarkConstraints/modern/phenomenology.py:23}.  The legacy default
inputs are \(\Delta F=2\) at \texttt{quarkConstraints/deltaf2.py:209}, with
\(B_d\) and \(B_s\) mixing evaluators at
\texttt{quarkConstraints/deltaf2.py:903} and
\texttt{quarkConstraints/deltaf2.py:922}.  The only live lepton-flavor routine
found is \(\mu\to e\gamma\) at \texttt{flavorConstraints/muToEGamma.py:75}.
sections.implementation_difficulty_prose:
\textbf{HIGH.}  Implementing B023 requires a new \(\Delta B=1\)
\(b\to s\nu\bar{\nu}\) operator basis, RS matching for neutral-current
left- and right-handed coefficients, \(B\to K^*\) form-factor and polarization
treatment, and an upper-limit likelihood or validated CL reinterpretation.

anchors.anchor_string: Combined BR(B -> K* nu nubar) = 2.7e-5 90% CL

matches.context:
L21: Source-extracted combined-limit text from arXiv TeX source:
L22: The analysis combines charged and neutral modes by adding negative log
L23: likelihoods, scaling neutral modes by tau(B+)/tau(B0), and obtains
L24: B(B -> K* nu nubar) < 2.7 x 10^{-5} at 90% C.L.

L14: The paper combines lattice-QCD and light-cone-sum-rule form factors with
L15: complete two-loop electroweak corrections to obtain
L16: BR(B+ -> K+ nu nubar) = (4.0 +/- 0.5) x 10^{-6} and
L17: BR(B0 -> K*0 nu nubar) = (9.2 +/- 1.0) x 10^{-6}.
L18:
L19: Source-extracted model-dependence note:
L20: The analysis relates new-physics effects in b -> s nu nubar to b -> s l+l-
